name: Dark Matter Barrage
tips: Your top-end board wipe — at 7 mana it's the most expensive standard cast in the set and eats most of a 10-mana pool, so you're casting it on a turn where you've already built mana and Dark Matter over the preceding turns.

The damage scales with your Dark Matter POOL — a player resource both sides can see. Feed the pool over several turns (Shady Trade Deal, Dark Matter Stash, Illicit Shadow Stones, Feed the Shadow, Matter of Time) before you pull the trigger.
Clean-up spell against token swarms (Common Rats, Diodebots, Rat-conjured boards). Baseline 5 damage halves a 10🤍 token — with 5+ Dark Matter banked the barrage one-shots them outright.
The gain (+1 Dark Matter per friendly Dark Mage) means the pool you keep after the wipe is bigger than before — this card accelerates your own ramp even when it only partially clears the enemy side.
Against a single large enemy minion, Matter of Time (4 mana, same pool scaling, plus burn) is more mana-efficient. Save Barrage for turns where you're hitting three or more targets.
rulings: Damage is `5 + your Dark Matter pool` at resolution (after the react window closes). The opponent's pool does not contribute.
Damage resolves before the Dark Matter gain, so the newly-gained Dark Matter does NOT scale this cast's damage — it's banked for the next one.
Every enemy minion on the board takes the full amount — range, tribe, and element are irrelevant.

The gain credits YOUR pool: +1 Dark Matter per friendly [[Dark Mage]] on the board (element Dark AND tribe exactly Mage — Shadow Blaster, Eclipse Shade, Gargoyle Sorceress, Matter Possessed, Erebus). Ratchanter (Mage Rat) and Grave Caller (Mage Undead) do NOT count.
If you have zero Dark Mages on the board, the gain is a no-op — the damage still resolves and the spell still costs 7 mana.
trivia: At 7 mana with board-wipe-scale payoff, Dark Matter Barrage intentionally sits at the apex of the Dark curve — the 'what all this ramp was for' payoff for Dark Matter decks.
